scenario: You write a plug-in on the account entity that runs a create and update operation. Some of the users report that they experience business process errors while updating the account record. You need to debug the plug-in.
q: Which six actions should you perform in sequence? Move the appropriate actions from the list of possible actions to the answer area and arrange them in the correct order.
options: Browse the assembly location, select the plugin Dynamic-link library (DLL), and click on Start Execution.
Select the plug-in step and click on Start Profiling.
Select the plug-in and click on Start Profiling.
Select the plug-in and click on Debug.
Trigger the plug-in and download the error log file.
Attach the Pluginregistration.exe process with the plug-in code in Visual Studio.
Select the plug-in step and click on Debug and select the profile.
answer: Select the plug-in step and click on Start Profiling.
Trigger the plug-in and download the error log file.
Select the plug-in step and click on Debug and select the profile.
Attach the Pluginregistration.exe process with the plug-in code in Visual Studio.
Browse the assembly location, select the plugin Dynamic-link library (DLL), and click on Start Execution.
why: Debug a plug-in with the Plug-in Profiler in the Plug-in Registration Tool: Install the plug-in profiler; select the plug-in STEP and click Start Profiling; trigger the plug-in (reproduce the error) and download the error log/profile file; then select the plug-in step, click Debug and select the downloaded profile; attach the PluginRegistration.exe process to your plug-in code in Visual Studio (set breakpoints); and finally browse to the assembly location, select the plug-in DLL and click Start Execution to replay the profiled event and hit your breakpoints.
